name: Minimum Spanning Tree (Kruskal / Prim)
description: A minimum spanning tree connects all vertices of a weighted graph with least total edge weight by a greedy rule: Kruskal sorts edges and adds the lightest that does not form a cycle (using union-find), while Prim grows a tree by repeatedly adding the lightest edge leaving it, until every vertex is connected.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Weighted connected graph"]
    N2{"Greedy strategy?"}
    N3["Kruskal: sort edges"]
    N4["Prim: grow from vertex"]
    N5["Pick min-weight safe edge"]
    N6{"Creates a cycle?"}
    N7["Reject edge"]
    N8["Add edge to MST"]
    N9["Union / extend tree"]
    N10{"All vertices connected?"}
    N11["Minimum spanning tree"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 -->|kruskal| N3
    N2 -->|prim| N4
    N3 --> N5
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|yes| N7
    N7 -->|next edge| N5
    N6 -->|no| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 -->|no| N5
    N10 -->|yes| N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff

sources.title: On the shortest spanning subtree of a graph and the traveling salesman problem
sources.journal: Proceedings of the American Mathematical Society

sources.title: Shortest connection networks and some generalizations
